title: System and method for controlling wafer breakage during ingot slicing operations
abstract: A system (100) for slicing wafers from a monocrystalline semiconductor ingot (102) includes a wire saw (104), a bond beam (124), the monocrystalline semiconductor ingot (102), and two sacrificial disks (134, 136). The wire saw (104) includes a wire web (112) and wire guides (114) operable to drive the wire web (112) during a slicing operation. The bond beam (124) is connected to the wire saw (104). The wire saw (104) is operable to move the bond beam (124) in a movement direction towards the wire web (112) during the slicing operation to slice the wafers from the ingot (102). The ingot (102) includes longitudinal end faces (106, 108) and a circumferential edge (144) extending between the longitudinal end faces (106, 108). The ingot (102) is attached to the bond beam (124) along the circumferential edge (144). One sacrificial disk (134, 136) is positioned adjacent each of the longitudinal end faces (106, 108) of the ingot (102).
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS : 1 . A system for slicing wafers from a monocrystalline semiconductor ingot , the system comprising : a wire saw including a wire web and wire guides operable to drive the wire web during a slicing operation; a bond beam connected to the wire saw, the wire saw operable to move the bond beam in a movement direction towards the wire web during the slicing operation to slice the wafers from the ingot ; the monocrystalline semiconductor ingot comprising longitudinal end faces and a circumferential edge extending between the longitudinal end faces , the ingot attached to the bond beam along the circumferential edge ; and two sacri ficial disks , wherein one sacri ficial disk is positioned adj acent each of the longitudinal end faces of the ingot . 2 . The system of claim 1 , wherein the ingot has an outer ingot diameter and the sacri ficial disks each have an outer disk diameter that is smaller than the outer ingot diameter . 3 . The system of claim 2 , wherein each sacri ficial disk is axially of fset from the adj acent longitudinal end face . 4 . The system of claim 1 , wherein the ingot has an outer ingot diameter and the sacri ficial disks each have an outer disk diameter that is equal to the outer ingot diameter . 5. The system of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of semiconductor material. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of monocrystalline semiconductor material. 7. The system of claim 5, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of amorphous semiconductor material. 8. The system of claim 5, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of sintered semiconductor material. 9. The system of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein each sacrificial disk is adhered to the adjacent longitudinal end face with an adhesive. 10. The system of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein each sacrificial disk is adhered to the bond beam with an adhesive . 11. The system of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the monocrystalline semiconductor ingot is a monocrystalline silicon ingot. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the monocrystalline silicon ingot has a (110) crystal plane positioned perpendicular to the movement direction. 13. The system of claim 11 or claim 12, wherein the wafers sliced from the monocrystalline silicon ingot are (100) monocrystalline silicon wafers. 14. The system of any one of claims 1 to 6 or claims 9 to 13, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of monocrystalline silicon. 15. The system of claim 14, wherein at least one of the sacrificial disks has a (110) crystal plane positioned at an oblique angle to the movement direction. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the at least one of the sacrificial disks has the (110) crystal plane positioned at an angle of between 30Â° to 60Â° to the movement direction. 17. The system of claim 15, wherein the at least one of the sacrificial disks has the (110) crystal plane positioned at an angle of 45Â° to the movement direction. 18. The system of any one of claims 14 to 17, wherein each of the sacrificial disks has a (110) crystal plane positioned at an oblique angle to the movement direction. 19. The system of claim 18, wherein each of the sacrificial disks has the (110) crystal plane positioned at an angle of between 30Â° to 60Â° to the movement direction. 20. The system of claim 18, wherein each of the sacrificial disks has the (110) crystal plane positioned at an angle of 45Â° to the movement direction. 21. The system of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the sacrificial disks each have a thickness of greater than 500 pm . 22. The system of claim 21, wherein the sacrificial disks each have the thickness of greater than 800 pm. 23. The system of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the sacrificial disks each have a thickness of between 500 pm to 2000 pm. 24. The system of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the sacrificial disks each have a thickness of between 800 pm to 1600 pm. 25. The system of any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein the sacrificial disks operate to inhibit uncontrolled breakage of the wafers during the slicing operation. 26. A method of slicing wafers from a monocrystalline semiconductor ingot, the method comprising: attaching a circumferential edge of the ingot to a bond beam; positioning sacrificial disks adjacent longitudinal end faces of the ingot, wherein one sacrificial disk is positioned adjacent each of the longitudinal end faces; connecting the bond beam to a wire saw that includes a wire web; and performing a slicing operation on the ingot by operating the wire saw to drive the wire web and move the bond beam and the ingot in a movement direction towards the wire web to slice the wafers from the ingot. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein positioning the sacrificial disks adjacent the longitudinal end faces comprises adhering each sacrificial disk to the adjacent longitudinal end face with an adhesive. 28. The method of claim 26 or claim 27, wherein positioning the sacrificial disks adjacent the longitudinal end faces comprises adhering the sacrificial disks to the bond beam with an adhesive. 29. The method of any one of claims 26 to 28, wherein the ingot has an outer ingot diameter and the sacrificial disks each have an outer disk diameter that is smaller than the outer ingot diameter. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein each sacrificial disk is axially offset from the adjacent longitudinal end face . 31. The method of any one of claims 26 to 28, wherein the ingot has an outer ingot diameter and the sacrificial disks each have an outer disk diameter that is equal to the outer ingot diameter. 32. The method of any one of claims 26 to 31, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of semiconductor material. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of monocrystalline semiconductor material. 34. The method of claim 32, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of amorphous semiconductor material. 35. The method of claim 32, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of sintered semiconductor material. 36. The method of any one of claims 26 to 35, wherein the monocrystalline semiconductor ingot is a monocrystalline silicon ingot. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein attaching the circumferential edge of the ingot to the bond beam comprises positioning a (110) crystal plane of the monocrystalline silicon ingot perpendicular to the movement direction. 38. The method of claim 36 or claim 37, wherein the wafers sliced from the ingot are (100) monocrystalline silicon wafers. 39. The method of any one of claims 26 to 33 or claims 36 to 38, wherein the sacrificial disks are made of monocrystalline silicon. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein positioning the sacrificial disks adjacent the longitudinal end faces of the ingot comprises positioning a (110) crystal plane of at least one of the sacrificial disks at an oblique angle to the movement direction. 41. The method of claim 40, wherein the (110) crystal plane of the at least one of the sacrificial disks is positioned at an angle of between 30Â° to 60Â° to the movement direction . 42. The method of claim 40, wherein the (110) crystal plane of the at least one of the sacrificial disks is positioned at an angle of 45Â° to the movement direction. 43. The method of any one of claims 39 to 42, wherein positioning the sacrificial disks adjacent the longitudinal end faces of the ingot comprises positioning a (110) crystal plane of each of the sacrificial disks at an oblique angle to the movement direction. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein the (110) crystal plane of each of the sacrificial disks is positioned at an angle of between 30Â° to 60Â° to the movement direction. 45. The method of claim 43, wherein the (110) crystal plane of each of the sacrificial disks is positioned at an angle of 45Â° to the movement direction. 46. The method of any one of claims 26 to 45, wherein the sacrificial disks each have a thickness of greater than 500 gm . 47. The method of claim 46, wherein the sacrificial disks each have the thickness of greater than 800 gm. 48. The method of any one of claims 26 to 45, wherein the sacrificial disks each have a thickness of between 500 gm to 2000 gm. 49. The method of any one of claims 26 to 45, wherein the sacrificial disks each have a thickness of between 800 gm to 1600 gm. 50. The method of any one of claims 26 to 49, wherein the sacrificial disks operate to inhibit uncontrolled breakage of the wafers during the slicing operation.
